privileged_info_summary: All five classic books in the corpus are real, publicly accessible texts from Project Gutenberg. Word count ranges in metadata are accurate: Pride and Prejudice contains approximately 130,415 words (range 115000-135000), Moby Dick contains 215,845 words (range 200000-230000), Sherlock Holmes contains 107,558 words (range 100000-115000), Frankenstein contains 78,106 words (range 70000-85000), and Alice in Wonderland contains 29,569 words (range 25000-35000). All paths are consistent across task scripts. The pass threshold is set to 60 points. Data is downloaded from authoritative Project Gutenberg URLs during task setup.
